extraction.one_line_summary: VALVERDE won stage 2 by timing his sprint perfectly into a headwind after Mitchelton-Scott's aggressive climbing pace failed to deliver for IMPEY
tactical_patterns: Mitchelton-Scott with POWER leading pulled hard on cat-2 climb to shed sprinters but IMPEY still lost sprint, wasted domestique effort
VALVERDE held 5th-6th wheel through headwind finish then timed kick perfectly while others started too early into wind
MUEHLBERGER doing leadout into headwind for BORA allowed VALVERDE to draft then jump, roadbook reconnaissance beats early power
Technical 90-degree turn at 1.5km plus right-hander near flamme rouge created toothpaste effect favoring positioned riders over pure speed

rider_signals.HODEG Alvaro: negative: dropped on relatively shallow 4.8% average climb after winning stage 1, cannot sustain multiple days
rider_signals.IMPEY Daryl: negative: Mitchelton-Scott sacrificed POWER to thin field for him on climb but still finished only 7th in sprint
rider_signals.POWER Robert: positive: pulled aggressively on cat-2 climb for IMPEY, showing classics specialist can do sustained climbing work
rider_signals.MCCARTHY Jay: positive: took 3rd after claiming he'd contest if possible, punches above pure sprinter weight in reduced groups
rider_signals.MUEHLBERGER Gregor: positive: Austrian led out into headwind finishing 2nd, showing Bora's depth beyond pure sprinters on climbing stages
